output:
A typical person would answer **Yes**.

The reasoning most people would apply: Mr. Wallace's involvement in organized crime ("the crime life") was the underlying cause that set both lethal chains in motion — Mr. Vincent poisoned him because of his position in organized crime, and Mr. Bruce ran him off the road because he was an associate of a rival in organized crime. Without the crime life, neither the poisoning nor the car attack would have occurred. Most people intuitively treat "the crime life" as a distal/background cause that made both proximate causes possible, so they'd say yes, it caused his death.
